title: Without Vision
description: Where a visual mode of operation is provided, ICT shall provide at least one mode of operation that does not require user vision.

title: With Limited Vision
description: Where a visual mode of operation is provided, ICT shall provide at least one mode of operation that magnifies, reduces glare, and provides contrast.

title: Without Perception of Color
description: Where a visual mode of operation is provided, ICT shall provide at least one visual mode of operation that does not require user perception of color.

title: Without Hearing
description: Where an audible mode of operation is provided, ICT shall provide at least one mode of operation that does not require user hearing.

title: With Limited Hearing
description: Where an audible mode of operation is provided, ICT shall provide at least one mode of operation that enhances audio.

title: With Limited Manipulation
description: Where a manual mode of operation is provided, ICT shall provide at least one mode of operation that does not require fine motor control or simultaneous manual operations.

title: Application Accessibility
description: Software applications shall conform to WCAG 2.0 Level AA and shall comply with applicable hardware accessibility requirements.

title: Accessibility Services
description: Where software is intended to run on a platform with accessibility services, the software shall interoperate with the accessibility services.

title: Electronic Support Documentation
description: Documentation in electronic format, including web-based self-service support, shall conform to WCAG 2.0 Level AA.

title: Operable Parts
description: Where provided, operable parts used in the normal operation of ICT shall comply with operable-parts requirements (one-handed operation, distinguishable by touch, accessible reach ranges).

title: Real-Time Text Functionality
description: Where ICT provides real-time, two-way voice communication, ICT shall provide real-time text functionality.
